text.div: Primary Problem: Roberta is a normally well-controlled Type 2 diabetic with well-managed hypertension. She experiences occassional gout attacks. Significant Clinical Event Requiring Patient Summary: Roberta has been been experiencing some neuropathy she is assuming is caused by her diabetes, and her gout is flaring up. She needs to see a GP in Yellowknife and may need a referral. Dr Allan GP (Prac ID 9600), Roberta's most responsible GP in Ontario, is wanting to communicate Roberta's clinical information to Dr Smith (Prac ID 2222) a GP in Alberta where Roberta is working.
There is no plan of care because ...

coding.display: Adult vaccine product containing only tetanus + diphtheria low dose + acellular pertussis low dose + inactivated human poliovirus antigens (medicinal product)

resource.valueString: 5cm peptic ulcer present on the the anterior of the duodenum.

code.text: Upper abdomen with the administration of oral and intravenous contrast

coding.display: Vital signs, weight, height, head circumference, oxygen saturation and BMI panel

valueCodeableConcept.text: Home has not been outfitted with accessibility devices

note.text: Roberta has episodic struggles with mobility and her home has not been outfitted with a ramp
Father died of a Heart Attack
